title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement codeofconduct@damurka.com. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired [Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder][https://github.com/mozilla/inclusion]. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Data Dimensions
text: DHIS2 offers three fixed dimensions: Data Elements (dx): Represent specific data points like cases, deaths, enrollments. Periods (pe): Represent timeframes like months, quarters, financial years. Organization Units (ou): Represent geographical locations organizational structures. Beyond fixed dimensions, can dynamically add additional dimensions : Categories: Group related data elements thematic analysis (e.g., age groups vaccination rates). Group Sets: Predefined sets data elements organization units efficient querying.

title: Data Dimensions
text: dimension set dimension items, specific data points within dimension. example: Period dimension items: Include ISO standard periods like 202401 January 2024 relative periods like LAST_WEEK. Organization unit dimension items: Include specific health facilities, districts, countries.

title: Data Dimensions
text: good news , don’t necessarily need know intricate details every dimension item building analytics queries! khisr provides functions handle complexities .

title: Data Dimensions
text: queries involving dates periods, relevant dimensions : Period dimension (pe): Use identifier various period formats (e.g., 2024Q1 first quarter 2024 LAST_6_MONTHS) filter data specific timeframes. Organization unit dimension (ou): Utilize keywords like USER_ORGUNIT USER_ORGUNIT_CHILDREN filter data based current location sub-units within organizational hierarchy.

title: Data Dimensions
text: deeper dives specific dimensions objects, refer comprehensive DHIS2 API documentation

title: Date and Period Format
text: date format use KHIS/DHIS2 API yyyy-MM-dd example, express March 20, 2014, must use 2014-03-20. period format described following table (also available [get_period_types()]):

title: Date and Period Format
text: can also utilize relative periods addition fixed periods (defined ). available relative period values : THIS_WEEK, LAST_WEEK, LAST_4_WEEKS, LAST_12_WEEKS, LAST_52_WEEKS, THIS_MONTH, LAST_MONTH, THIS_BIMONTH, LAST_BIMONTH, THIS_QUARTER, LAST_QUARTER, THIS_SIX_MONTH, LAST_SIX_MONTH, MONTHS_THIS_YEAR, QUARTERS_THIS_YEAR, THIS_YEAR, MONTHS_LAST_YEAR, QUARTERS_LAST_YEAR, LAST_YEAR, LAST_5_YEARS, LAST_10_YEARS, LAST_10_FINANCIAL_YEARS, LAST_12_MONTHS, LAST_3_MONTHS, LAST_6_BIMONTHS, LAST_4_QUARTERS, LAST_2_SIXMONTHS, THIS_FINANCIAL_YEAR, LAST_FINANCIAL_YEAR, LAST_5_FINANCIAL_YEARS

title: khisr
text: filter metadata several filter operations can applied returned list metadata. format filter straight-forward follows pattern property:operator:value, property property metadata want filter , operator comparison operator want perform value value check (operators require value).

what: Retrieve Data from Kenya Health Information System (KHIS)
title: Retrieve Data from Kenya Health Information System (KHIS)
text: khisr package designed seamlessly integrate Kenya Health Information System (KHIS), providing R users powerful interface efficient data retrieval. KHIS cornerstone health information management Kenya, khisr simplifies process accessing working KHIS data directly within R environment.

title: Retrieve Data from Kenya Health Information System (KHIS)
text: can install development version khisr like :
code: #install.packages('pak') pak::pak('damurka/khisr')

title: Retrieve Data from Kenya Health Information System (KHIS)
code: library("khisr")

title: Retrieve Data from Kenya Health Information System (KHIS)
text: khisr , default, help interact KHIS authenticated user. calling function makes API call need credentials KHIS. expected set credential download data. setting credential can invoke function download data API. overview, ’ve logged KHIS specific user hidden chunk.
code: # Set the credentials using username and password khis_cred(username = 'KHIS username', password = 'KHIS password')  # Set credentials using configuration path khis_cred(config_path = 'path/to/secret.json')

title: Retrieve Data from Kenya Health Information System (KHIS)
text: Please note khisr project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project, agree abide terms.

title: Analytics Data Dimensions — analytics-dimension
text: Constructs dimensions expression analytics queries based specified property, operator, values.

title: Analytics Data Dimensions — analytics-dimension
code: analytics_dimension(property, operator, values)  operator %.d% values  operator %.f% values

title: Analytics Data Dimensions — analytics-dimension
text: property character string representing whether dimension filter. accepts 'dimension', 'filter'. operator character string representing property filter (e.g., 'dx', 'pe', 'ou'). values vector values semi-colon separated string items.

title: Analytics Data Dimensions — analytics-dimension
text: spliced list filter format property=operator:value

title: Analytics Data Dimensions — analytics-dimension
text: DHIS2 organizes data using multiple dimensions, unique identifier set items represent specific data points within dimension. Data elements (dx): Indicators, data set reporting rate metrics, data element operands, program indicators, program data elements, program attributes, validation rules. Periods (pe): ISO periods (e.g., 202401) relative periods (e.g., LAST_WEEK). Organisation unit hierarchy (ou): Specific health facilities, districts, countries, keywords user location sub-units. Category option combinations (co): Category option combo identifiers. Attribute option combinations (ao): Category option combo identifiers. Categories: Category option identifiers. Data element group sets: Data element group identifiers. Organisation unit group sets: Organisation unit group identifiers. infix operator used filter dimension includes: %.d%: Infix operator constructing dimension filters. Equivalent calling analytics_dimension("dimension", ...). %.f%: Infix operator constructing filter filters. Equivalent calling analytics_dimension("filter", ...).

title: Analytics Data Dimensions — analytics-dimension
code: # Create a dimension for data element "DE_1234" analytics_dimension('dimension', "dx", "DE_1234") #> <spliced> #> $dimension #> [1] "dx:DE_1234" #>   # Equivalent to the expression above dx %.d% "DE_1234" #> <spliced> #> $dimension #> [1] "dx:DE_1234" #>   # Create a filter dimension for the period of January 2024 pe %.f% "202401" #> <spliced> #> $filter #> [1] "pe:202401" #>   # Create filter dimension for periods "202401" and "202402": analytics_dimension("filter", "pe", c("202401", "202402")) #> <spliced> #> $filter #> [1] "pe:202401;202402" #>

what: Retrieves Analytics Table Data from KHIS — get_analytics

code: get_analytics(   ...,   return_type = c("uid", "name"),   retry = 2,   verbosity = 0,   timeout = 60 )

title: Retrieves Analytics Table Data from KHIS — get_analytics
text: ... One metadata filters key-value pairs. return_type type return names uid. retry Number times retry API call case failure (defaults 2). verbosity Level HTTP information print call: 0: output 1: Show headers 2: Show headers bodies 3: Show headers, bodies, CURL status message. timeout Maximum number seconds wait API response.

what: Get Metadata from a KHIS — get_metadata
title: Get Metadata from a KHIS — get_metadata
text: get_metadata retrieves metadata specified endpoint KHIS

title: Get Metadata from a KHIS — get_metadata
code: get_metadata(   endpoint,   ...,   fields = c("id", "name"),   retry = 2,   verbosity = 0,   timeout = 60 )

title: Get Metadata from a KHIS — get_metadata
text: endpoint KHIS API endpoint metadata interest (e.g. dataElements, organisationUnits). ... One metadata filters key-value pairs. fields specific columns returned tibble. retry Number times retry API call case failure (defaults 2). verbosity Level HTTP information print call: 0: output 1: Show headers 2: Show headers bodies 3: Show headers, bodies, CURL status message. timeout Maximum number seconds wait API response.

title: Get Metadata from a KHIS — get_metadata
code: # Get the categories metadata get_metadata('categories') #> Error in req_perform(., verbosity = verbosity): Failed to perform HTTP request. #> Caused by error in `curl::curl_fetch_memory()`: #> ! Failed to connect to hiskenya.org port 443 after 103 ms: Connection refused  # Get the datasets metadata with fields 'id,name,organisationUnits' and filter # only the datasets with id 'WWh5hbCmvND' get_metadata('dataSets',              fields = 'id,name,organisationUnits[id,name,path]',              id %.eq% 'WWh5hbCmvND') #> Error in req_perform(., verbosity = verbosity): Failed to perform HTTP request. #> Caused by error in `curl::curl_fetch_memory()`: #> ! Failed to connect to hiskenya.org port 443 after 97 ms: Connection refused  # Get data elements filtered by dataElementgroups id get_metadata('dataElements',              dataElementGroups.id %.eq% 'IXd7DXxZqzL',              fields = ':all') #> Error in req_perform(., verbosity = verbosity): Failed to perform HTTP request. #> Caused by error in `curl::curl_fetch_memory()`: #> ! Failed to connect to hiskenya.org port 443 after 102 ms: Connection refused

Expect TRUE khis_has_cred() #> [1] TRUE  # Clear credentials khis_cred_clear()  # Check if credentials available. Expect FALSE khis_has_cred() #> [1] FALSE

title: Metadata Filter — metadata-filter
text: Formats metadata filter DHIS 2 equivalent.

title: Metadata Filter — metadata-filter
code: metadata_filter(   property,   operator,   values,   property_arg = caller_arg(property),   operator_arg = caller_arg(operator),   values_arg = caller_arg(values),   call = caller_env() )  property %.eq% values  property %.ieq% values  property %.~eq% values  property %.ne% values  property %.Like% values  property %.~Like% values  property %.^Like% values  property %.~^Like% values  property %.Like$% values  property %.~Like$% values  property %.like% values  property %.~like% values  property %.^like% values  property %.~^like% values  property %.like$% values  property %.~like$% values  property %.gt% values  property %.ge% values  property %.lt% values  property %.le% values  property %.token% values  property %.~token% values  property %.in% values  property %.~in% values

title: Metadata Filter — metadata-filter
text: property property metadata want filter operator comparison operator want perform values value check property_arg description operator_arg description values_arg description call description

title: Metadata Filter — metadata-filter
text: spliced list filter format property:operator:value

title: Metadata Filter — metadata-filter
text: filter metadata several filter operations can applied returned list metadata. format filter straight-forward follows pattern property:operator:value, property property metadata want filter , operator comparison operator want perform value value check (operators require value). view operator see DHIS 2 Operator %.eq%        - Equality %.ieq%       - Case insensitive string, match exact %.~eq%       - Inequality %.ne%        - Inequality %.Like%      - Case sensitive string, match anywhere %.~Like%     - Case sensitive string, match anywhere %.^Like%     - Case sensitive string, match start %.~^Like%    - Case sensitive string, match start %.Like$%     - Case sensitive string, match end %.~Like$%    - Case sensitive string, match end %.like%      - Case insensitive string, match anywhere %.~like%     - Case insensitive string, match anywhere %.^like%    - Case insensitive string, match start %.~^like%    - Case insensitive string, match start %.like$%     - Case insensitive string, match end %.~like$%    - Case insensitive string, match end %.gt%        - Greater %.ge%        - Greater equal %.lt%        - Less %.le%        - Less equal %.token%     - Match multiple tokens search property %.~token%    - match multiple tokens search property %.%        - Find objects matching 1 values %.~%       - Find objects matching 1 values

title: Metadata Filter — metadata-filter
code: # Generate an equality filter id %.eq% 'element_id' #> <spliced> #> $filter #> [1] "id:eq:element_id" #>   # Finding multiple ids 'id' %.in% c('id1', 'id2', 'id3') #> <spliced> #> $filter #> [1] "id:in:[id1,id2,id3]" #>   # Get all data elements which have a data set with id ID1 'dataSetElements.dataSet.id' %.eq% 'ID1' #> <spliced> #> $filter #> [1] "dataSetElements.dataSet.id:eq:ID1" #>   # get data elements which are members of the ANC data element group 'dataElementGroups.id' %.eq% 'qfxEYY9xAl6' #> <spliced> #> $filter #> [1] "dataElementGroups.id:eq:qfxEYY9xAl6" #>   # Get data elements which have any option set metadata_filter('optionSet', '!null', NULL) #> <spliced> #> $filter #> [1] "optionSet:!null" #>
